channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Los Angeles deputy mayor allegedly made bomb threat to City Hall
item.description: Federal agents searched the home of Los Angeles Deputy Mayor Brian Williams on Tuesday following allegations that he made a bomb threat to City Hall earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the US. Here's what we know
item.description: A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey and across the eastern U.S., sparking speculation and concern over where they came from and why.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illinois man fixes bikes for the less fortunate
item.description: "Growing up as a kid, I never had a new bike, but I did have a bike," said Chuck's Bikes and Blessings owner Chuck Henderson.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the most Googled Christmas cookie recipes in every state
item.description: Google Trends released a map of the most-searched-for cookies by state, and the recipe that's most popular in your state might just surprise you.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, Fox 5

item.title: Major discount retailer begins 'going out of business' sales across US
item.description: After filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection and closing hundreds of stores, Big Lots is preparing to close all of its locations, the company confirmed on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government, or federal agencies will shutter.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker said it is “debatable” whether transgender women will “always” have a physical advantage over cisgender, or non-transgender, women.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: New Year's Eve ball drop numbers unveiled in NYC
item.description: Even though New Year’s Eve is nearly two weeks away, there was an official celebration in the location best known worldwide for ringing in the new year.
item.dc:creator: James Ford, Fox 5

item.title: Advocates train immigrants to 'prepare to stay' in the US under Trump
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Winston Leiva rattles off a long list of things immigrants should do to protect themselves against President-elect Donald Trump's promise to conduct mass deportations when he returns to the White House. Make a plan for someone to care for your children if you are arrested.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance meets with Johnson after Trump torpedoes spending bill
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance met with Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Wednesday night, hours after President-elect Trump torpedoed the GOP leader’s spending deal.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: France rape trial: Gisèle Pelicot speaks out after ex-husband, 50 others found guilty
item.description: Gisèle Pelicot spoke of her “very difficult ordeal” after 51 men were all found guilty Thursday in the drugging-and-rape trial that turned her into a feminist hero, expressing support for other victims of sexual violence whose cases don't get such attention and “whose stories remain untold.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Fox 5

item.title: Harmful gas billowing from Texas and New Mexico comes mostly from smaller leaks, researchers say
item.description: The blob on the satellite image is a rainbow of colors. An analyst digitally sharpens it and there, highlighted in red, is the source: a concrete oil pad spewing methane.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In Florida, a race is on to save the Everglades and protect a key source of drinking water
item.description: For decades, largescale engineering projects for development and agriculture drained and partitioned South Florida's Everglades, a vast wetlands landscape home to endangered and threatened species and a vital source of drinking water for millions of Floridians.
item.dc:creator: Dorany Pineda And Rebecca Blackwell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The egg industry kills 350 million male chicks a year. New tech offers an alternative
item.description: Every year the U.S. egg industry kills about 350 million male chicks because, while the fuzzy little animals are incredibly cute, they will never lay eggs, so have little monetary value.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Congress battles over looming government shutdown and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The US egg industry kills 350 million chicks a year. New technology offers an alternative
item.description: WILTON, Iowa (AP) — Every year the U.S. egg industry kills about 350 million male chicks because, while the fuzzy little animals are incredibly cute, they will never lay eggs, so have little monetary value.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How New York prosecutors used a terrorism law in the charges against Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York prosecutors are using a 9/11-era anti-terrorism law in their case against the man charged with gunning down UnitedHealthcare's CEO outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California man who messaged Wisconsin shooter, planned own attack ordered to surrender guns
item.description: The shooter who killed a student and teacher at a religious school in Wisconsin brought two guns to the school and was in contact with a man in California who authorities say was planning to attack a government building, according to authorities and court documents that became public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Medical examiner identifies victims killed in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: Officials have released new details about the shooting at a Madison, Wisconsin school that claimed the life of a student and teacher, and wounded six others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey military base confirms multiple drone contraband-smuggling incursions this year
item.description: A military base in New Jersey said it has experienced multiple drone incursions related to the smuggling of contraband into a federal prison on site.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani ordered to appear at contempt hearing in January over failure to give up assets
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday ordered Rudy Giuliani to appear in court in early January to face claims that he should be held in contempt for failing to turn over assets including a New York apartment lease and a signed Joe DiMaggio shirt to two Georgia election poll workers who won
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hochul deploys hundreds of National Guard members to NYC subway system
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul recently announced the deployment of 750 National Guard members to watch over the New York City subway system, as the holiday tourist season ramps up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man accused of running secret Chinese gov police station in NYC makes plea: US Attorney
item.description: One of two New York City residents accused of operating a secret Chinese police station in Chinatown pleaded guilty in federal court to conspiring to act as an agent of China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 184-foot floating Christmas tree collapses, killing 21-year-old man
item.description: A 21-year-old man was killed when a 184-foot floating Christmas tree collapsed in Brazil earlier this week. The incident occurred on Monday in the city of Maricá, which is located roughly 25 miles east of Rio de Janeiro.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: First severe bird flu case confirmed in US: CDC
item.description: Human cases of bird flu have been reported in the U.S. before, but infections were mild in those patients.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, WGN 9

item.title: Louisiana patient has first severe bird flu case in US: health officials
item.description: Human cases of bird flu have been reported in the U.S. before, but infections were mild in those patients.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, KRON4

item.title: GOP report recommends Liz Cheney be criminally investigated over Jan. 6 work
item.description: It’s a claim that if pursued would likely face significant roadblocks but that nonetheless comes as Trump has suggested members of the Jan. 6 panel should “go to jail” for their work.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans butt heads on daylight saving time after Trump directive
item.description: A Republican brouhaha is emerging after President-elect Trump called to eliminate daylight saving time, setting up battle lines between prominent GOP lawmakers in their hopes to lock the clock. Trump waded into the longtime sore subject Friday, surprising — and perplexing — some lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Grubhub to pay $25M to settle claims of tricking customers and drivers
item.description: Federal Trade Commission officials said Grubhub engaged in illegal practices by misleading customers about the cost of delivery and the benefits of a Grubhub subscription.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster for 'election interference' after pre-election poll
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump sued the Des Moines Register and its pollster for “brazen election interference” in publishing a survey the weekend before the election that showed Democrat Kamala Harris with a surprising lead of three percentage points in the state. The Register's parent Gannett Co.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taco Bell adding chicken nuggets to menus nationwide
item.description: Taco Bell, known for its Mexican-inspired cuisine, will begin serving its take on chicken nuggets later this month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Judge allows testing of tissue from George Floyd as officer convicted of killing him seeks new trial
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A judge has granted permission to lawyers for Derek Chauvin to have samples from George Floyd examined as part of the former Minneapolis police officer's efforts to challenge his conviction on a federal civil rights charge stemming from Floyd's death in 2020. U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Event promoters, hotels and lodging sites soon will have to disclose extra fees up front
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission on Tuesday said it will soon require hotels, vacation rental platforms and live event promoters to disclose any fees up front when they list prices.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about the victims in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: A 14-year-old girl and a substitute teacher were killed in Monday's shooting. Another teacher and five students were injured.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Is that a drone or a plane? Experts help explain the differences
item.description: Up in the sky, is that a drone, a plane or a helicopter? Experts who study unmanned aircraft systems — better known as drones — say it can be tough to tell from miles away. But there are clues.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5
